transfer.question: Hello, I ordered the "Amazing Hand" DIY kit from you and have now assembled it. One of the 3-pin break out boards (SCS-Y4-2012) was unfortunately defective when it arrived. There...
transfer.answer:
Dear Anders,
Thank you so much for your detailed feedback and for your kind words about the "Amazing Hand" DIY kit!
We sincerely apologize for the defective 3-pin breakout board. Thank you for bringing this short circuit issue to our attention. We highly value your suggestion and will definitely strengthen our quality assurance checks on these specific parts in the future to prevent this from happening again.
We hope you have a lot of fun playing with your new robotic hand.
Please stay tuned to our updates, as we are working on more exciting new products that you might also enjoy.

transfer.question: Dear Seeed Studio Technical Support Team, We recently purchased your reComputer Mini J501 Carrier Board for Jetson AGX Orin™ with GMSL Extension, and we are currently evaluating ca...
transfer.answer:
HI,

At the moment, we have not adapted ZED to our GMSL solution. Supporting a new GMSL configuration is a fairly complex process, so we do not have any guidance documentation available.
If your order volume is significant, we can offer a paid customization service for the new GMSL adaptation. The ZED X One camera itself uses a GMSL2 + Fakra interface, and the reComputer mini J501 uses the MAX96712 deserializer, which is also a four-channel solution. Therefore, in theory, this adaptation should be feasible as a paid custom project.

param_query.question: Hello, Thanks. Indeed at lower power mode it doesn't appear. In MAXN, it appears when I load Yolo26X which is big and draws a lot of current. When a warning about overcurrent throt...
param_query.answer:
Hello,
Thanks for the update.
This largely depends on your working environment. In a typical indoor setup, our heatsink/cooling solution can generally handle the MAXN power/thermal load, so the system should operate normally.
The MAXN warning is a system-level detection message. On older Jetson developer kits, the stock cooling is often not sufficient to sustain MAXN under heavy loads, so the warning can appear more easily. If your CPU temperature remains within a normal range and you are not observing actual performance drops, you can generally ignore the warning.

compatibility.question: Good Morning, I hope this message finds you well. I have a Seeed Studio reComputer J3011. According to the datasheet, it supports a 16-pin CSI camera interface. I am using a TI DS9...
compatibility.answer:
Hi,

I think you might have checked the wrong document. Our datasheet specifies a 15-pin CSI interface.

1. The reComputer J3011 carrier board comes with native 15-pin CSI interfaces (2 independent 2-lane MIPI CSI-2 interfaces), and there is no native 16-pin CSI physical interface.

2. RAW10/RAW12 formats are fully supported.

3. Standard CSI-2 signaling (HS/LP modes) is fully supported.

We do not provide a dedicated document for this; only the schematic diagram is available for reference.Find it under Resources on the wiki page.
Getting start with reComputer J30/40 | Seeed Studio Wiki

compatibility.question: QuestionI replied to the tech support mail, but I couldn't got the answer yet. The support number is ##339759##. hky6385 22 Jan, 2:55 PM I replied to the tech support mail, but I couldn't got the answer yet. The support number is ##339759##.
compatibility.answer:
Hi,

NVIDIA recently made a hardware change to the DRAM used on Orin modules. The related PCN is available here:
https://developer.nvidia.com/embedded/jetson-pcn-center#jetson-pcn:~:text=Other%20Orin%20PCNs-,Orin%20DRAM%20PCNs,-The%20table%20below

If you are using a newer batch of modules, this change may cause flashing to fail.

We expect to integrate the PCN patch into our BSP today and will provide you with a new BSP download link for reflashing as soon as it is released.

In addition, could you please send us a photo of the exterior casing of your reComputer unit for reference?

compatibility.question: Dear Technical Support Team, You have received a technical support inquiry from a customer. Please respond to the customer as soon as possible using the provided customer email add...
compatibility.answer:
Hello,
Thanks for your question.
The Robotics J401 carrier board specifies a power input range of 19–54V via the XT30 (2+2) connector. A “48V” lithium battery pack typically reaches ~54.6V when fully charged (e.g., a 13S Li-ion/LiPo pack). Since 54.6V exceeds the stated 54V maximum, we do not recommend connecting the battery directly at full charge.
Even a small over-voltage margin can become a reliability risk, and in real robotics systems you can also see additional transient spikes during load changes, cable inductance effects, motor start/stop, etc.

howto.question: QuestionDear Sir, In our application, we used this Jetson, we connected a camera (5W) and a wireless keyboard and mouse to the USB ports. After that, we got the message "system throttled due to over-current." Can you explain what's wrong and what I n...
howto.answer:
Dear Line,

The camera, mouse, and keyboard you have connected should not cause the device to enter an overcurrent protection state.

My initial guess is that the notification you're seeing might be a system message indicating that the device is running at a high temperature.
Please help confirming three things:
1. Your device model (SKU would be better) and the JetPack version installed on the device (cat /etc/nv_tegra_release)
2. A photo of the notification when it appears
3. If convenient, please use the jtop tool to verify whether the device is actually experiencing frequency throttling.

Please do not attempt to run apt upgrade or click the system update button in the GUI on third-party Jetson carrier boards. In some cases, even running dpkg --configure -a may cause NVIDIA L4T-related packages to be replaced, which could result in the system being unintentionally upgraded to version 36.4.7.
This image has been fully validated by us and is confirmed to be JetPack 6.1 (L4T 36.4.0)：

howto.question: Hello, I've noticed that the aluminum heatsink with fan for Jetson Orin NX/Orin Nano/Xavier NX modules is only specified for use up to a humidity level of 65%. What would be the co...
howto.answer:
Hi,

Thank you for your consultation. Below is the overview regarding the use of this aluminum heat dissipation module under 85% humidity conditions:
Key Risks:
1. The service life of the fan may be shortened by over 50%, with a high likelihood of bearing corrosion and motor failure.

2.The oxidation rate of the heat sink will accelerate, resulting in a 5–10% reduction in heat dissipation efficiency.

3.The risk of condensation will increase significantly, which may cause short circuits in the Jetson module.

general.question: 我希望您能帮我澄清一些关于系统通信协议的问题。我计划连接几个Feetech电机（TTL协议）和一些Robstride电机（CAN协议），通过Jetson驱动它们。虽然有人建议用USB设备控制电机，但我读到通过GPIO引脚直接连接可以显著提升通信速度。请问这些引脚的位置，以及同时控制这两种电机需要什么？用recomputer j30/j40载板的话   Interfaces Usage | Seeed Studio Wiki
general.answer:
Hi,

thank you for your message. Regarding your question, our suggestions are as follows:
On the reComputer J401 carrier board:

- For Feetech TTL motors: Use the onboard UART (3.3V). Since the motor TX is 5V, a 5V→3.3V level shifter is required.
- For Robstride CAN motors: Use the onboard CAN interface directly; no USB-CAN adapter is needed.

Do not use GPIO for simulated communication, as its stability and speed are inferior to hardware UART/CAN.
The two interfaces can be used at the same time without conflict.

troubleshooting.question: I can't get past the registration, can you help me? My AI is-seeed studio reComputer Industrial J4012- Fanless Edge AI Device with Jetson Orin NX 16GB I entered the password, it gi...
troubleshooting.answer:
Thank you for contacting Seeed Studio.
To better understand the issue, could you please help clarify the following?
When you power on the device, has the system already been configured with a username and password, or are you still in the initial setup stage where the system asks you to create a username and password for the first time?
Also, regarding the code you mentioned, could you please share a screenshot or photo of the screen showing the code? This will help us better understand what step the system is currently at and provide more accurate assistance.
We look forward to your reply.

troubleshooting.question: Dear Seeed Studio Technical Support Team, I am writing to urgently request assistance with my reComputer J4012 (Orin NX 16GB) device. I am unable to flash the system using the offi...
troubleshooting.answer:
Dear Arda Nas,
Hello, regarding your issue, we have tested flashing the reComputer J4012 (Orin NX 16GB) using the official JetPack 6.0 image from our Wiki, and we were not able to reproduce the problem you described.
We kindly suggest that you completely power off the Jetson (disconnect the power), then re-enter Recovery Mode and try flashing again.
If the issue still persists after retrying, the device may require further inspection. In that case, we can only suggest sending the unit back to us for repair.
